member.name: M:PrivateKeyECDSA.GenerateInternal
member.summary: Create a new private ECDSA key.

member.name: M:PrivateKeyECDSA.FromPrivateKeyInfoInternal(PrivateKeyInfo)
member.summary: Create a new private key from a private key ino object.

member.name: M:PrivateKeyECDSA.FromECPrivateKeyInternal(ECPrivateKeyStructure)
member.summary: Create a new private key from a ECPrivateKey object.

member.name: M:PrivateKeyECDSA.FromBytesInternal(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Create a private key from a byte array.

member.name: M:PrivateKeyECDSA.LegacyDeriveChildKey(System.Byte[],System.Int64)
member.summary: Throws an exception when trying to derive a child key.

member.name: M:PrivateKeyECDSA.FromSeed(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Create an ECDSA key from seed. Implement the published algorithm as defined in BIP32 in order to derive the primary account key from the original (and never stored) master key. The original master key, which is a secure key generated according to the BIP39 specification, is input to this operation, and provides the base cryptographic seed material required to ensure the output is sufficiently random to maintain strong cryptographic assurances. The fromSeed() method must be provided with cryptographically secure material; otherwise, it will produce insecure output.

member.name: M:PrivateKeyECDSA.DerivableKeyECDSA(System.Byte[])
member.summary: Create a derived key. The industry standard protocol for deriving an active ECDSA keypair from a BIP39 master key is described in BIP32. By using this deterministic mechanism to derive cryptographically secure keypairs from a single original secret, the user maintains secure access to their wallet, even if they lose access to a particular system or wallet local data store. The active keypair can always be re-derived from the original master key. The use of the fixed "key" values in this code is defined by this deterministic protocol, and this data is mixed, in a deterministic but cryptographically secure manner, with the original master key and/or other derived keys "higher" in the tree to produce a cryptographically secure derived key. This "Key Derivation Function" makes use of secure hash algorithm and a secure hash based message authentication code to produce an initialization vector, and then produces the actual key from a portion of that vector.

member.name: M:PrivateKeyECDSA.BigIntTo32Bytes(BigInteger)
member.summary: Create a big int byte array.
